# Pinned upper bounds prevent silent breakage if a future major release
# changes the cryptography or jcs API. Bump deliberately when verifying
# the fixture round-trip still produces 7/7 OK against the golden vectors.
#
# cryptography floor is 46.0.7 because the lower 42.x-46.0 range is
# vulnerable to three advisories that all land in 46.0.7:
#   - GHSA-r6ph-v2qm-q3c2 (HIGH)  SECT-curve subgroup attack (fixed 46.0.5)
#   - GHSA-p423-j2cm-9vmq (MOD)   non-contiguous buffer overflow (fixed 46.0.7)
#   - GHSA-m959-cc7f-wv43 (LOW)   incomplete DNS name constraint (fixed 46.0.6)
# Ed25519 verify is API-stable across 42.x-46.x so the bump is drop-in.
cryptography>=46.0.7,<47.0.0
jcs>=0.2.1,<1.0.0
